title: C++ Introduction
content:
### What is C++?

C++ is a general-purpose programming language created by Bjarne Stroustrup as an extension of the C programming language. It was first released in 1985 and has since become one of the most widely used programming languages in the world.

---

### Key Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Object-Oriented | Supports classes, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation |
| Performance | Compiled language with zero-cost abstractions |
| Portability | Runs on virtually any platform |
| Rich Standard Library | STL provides containers, algorithms, iterators |
| Low-Level Access | Pointers, memory management, hardware access |

---

### C++ Standards Evolution

| Standard | Year | Key Features |
|----------|------|--------------|
| C++98 | 1998 | First ISO standard, STL, templates |
| C++11 | 2011 | Auto, lambdas, move semantics, smart pointers |
| C++14 | 2014 | Generic lambdas, constexpr improvements |
| C++17 | 2017 | Filesystem, parallel algorithms, structured bindings |
| C++20 | 2020 | Concepts, coroutines, ranges, modules, spans |
| C++23 | 2023 | `std::print`, `std::expected`, stacktrace library |
| C++26 | 2026 | Reflection, pattern matching, contracts (planned) |

---

> **Key Point:** C++ is a compiled, statically-typed language that gives programmers fine-grained control over system resources and memory.

quiz.question: Who created the C++ programming language?
